symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical proximal muscle aching and severe weakness (thighs, buttocks, shoulders)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical proximal aching and weakness is the classic distribution of statin muscle toxicity.
A symmetrical proximal muscular pattern without a sensory level is not typical of cord compression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset weeks after an atorvastatin dose increase/doubling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tight temporal link between a statin dose increase and symmetrical proximal muscle symptoms is characteristic of dose-dependent statin muscle toxicity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Concurrent clarithromycin (potent CYP3A4 inhibitor) drug-drug interaction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4 and markedly increases atorvastatin exposure, potentiating muscle toxicity.
The interaction sharply raises statin levels and thereby the risk of severe muscle breakdown.
Absence of visibly dark urine is reassuring but does not exclude myoglobinuria; a urine dipstick and blood CK are still required.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sensory loss, no upper-motor-neuron signs, no bladder/bowel disturbance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No sensory level, no UMN signs and no sphincter disturbance argue strongly against a spinal cord cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive functional decline - now needs help for all transfers (fall risk realised)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe, progressive functional loss reflects the severity of the muscle injury and mandates urgent objective assessment.
Progressive, profound proximal weakness that persists despite stopping the statin would raise this diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Markedly elevated creatine kinase (CK) on blood testing

loinc_ref.display: Creatine kinase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated CK; a marked rise (e.g. >10x, often >40x the upper limit of normal) with renal impairment indicates rhabdomyolysis, whereas a mild rise supports benign SAMS
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity cannot be graded and rhabdomyolysis is neither confirmed nor excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A very high CK with renal impairment is the key laboratory determinant of rhabdomyolysis.
A raised CK in the context of a statin dose increase supports statin muscle injury and grades its severity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Urine myoglobin positive (dipstick blood-positive without red cells) / renal impairment

investigation_details.investigation_name: Urine myoglobin dipstick and renal function
investigation_details.expected_result: Myoglobinuria (dipstick positive for blood without red cells on microscopy) and/or a rise in creatinine indicate rhabdomyolysis with kidney involvement
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Myoglobinuric acute kidney injury may be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Myoglobinuria with renal impairment confirms rhabdomyolysis and drives emergency management.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Severe statin-associated muscle symptoms (dose increase plus CYP3A4-inhibitor interaction)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Symmetrical proximal aching/weakness beginning after an atorvastatin dose increase, with concurrent clarithromycin raising statin exposure, indicates severe statin muscle toxicity - withhold the statin, review the interacting antibiotic, hydrate, and arrange urgent CK/renal testing.
Profound/progressive weakness with a markedly raised CK and myoglobinuria/renal impairment defines rhabdomyolysis and mandates emergency care - the discrimination the case tests.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Grades muscle injury and, with renal function, discriminates benign SAMS from rhabdomyolysis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Renal function (U&E/creatinine) and a urine myoglobin dipstick
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects myoglobinuric acute kidney injury, the dangerous complication of severe statin muscle breakdown.
Baseline assessment when reviewing statin toxicity and before considering any future lipid therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Medication review of the atorvastatin-clarithromycin interaction
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies and removes the avoidable CYP3A4-inhibitor driver of raised statin exposure.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance for mild statin myalgia with normal function and no red flags
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Over-triage; uncomplicated statin myalgia without rhabdomyolysis red flags is managed by withholding the drug and urgent (not emergency) bloods, reserving emergency care for dark urine, profound/progressive weakness or vomiting.
